pattern: With such a tiny window of candidates around n, simply testing every positive integer within k of n against the bitwise-disjoint condition and summing the ones that qualify is fast enough.
hints: The only candidates worth testing are the positive integers within a distance of k from n, since anything farther away automatically fails the distance condition.
For each candidate in that small window, check whether n and the candidate share no set bits in common by testing if their bitwise AND is zero.
Add up every candidate in the window that passes the bitwise-AND check and is a positive integer.

edge_inputs.description: three compatible integers within the window
no compatible integers exist in the window
the smallest possible n and k
the largest allowed n and k
a window that would include zero or negative candidates if the positivity check were missed
n itself shares bits with every value near it, filtering out most of the window
a power of two n leaves many disjoint bit patterns available nearby

stress.description: random n and k across the full bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 100)
    k = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [n, k]
